solution.p: Since we are not allowed to divide by zero, the denominator of \displaystyle{G} cannot be zero. To find what x values will make the denominator zero, we solve the equation:
We need to take 0 and -3 out of the functions domain, because they will cause the denominator to be zero, and we cannot divide by zero. The domain of G is:
We could also use set notation to represent the domain:
